name: Seedance Video Generator
description: Generate AI videos using Seedance 2.0 (ByteDance) — supports text-to-video, first-frame, first+last frame, and reference image modes

inputs.label: Prompt
inputs.placeholder: Describe the video you want to create, e.g. 'A woman walking on the beach at sunset, cinematic slow motion, golden hour lighting'

inputs.label: Last Frame Image
inputs.placeholder: Paste image URL for the ending frame (optional)

agentInstructions:
Generate a video using the Seedance 2.0 video generation tool (sd_generate_video).

Parameter mapping:
- "Prompt" → prompt parameter (keep concise, under 500 chars — describe the visual scene briefly)
- "First Frame Image" → first_frame_image parameter. The user may upload a photo from their library (sent as base64 data URI in images array) OR paste a URL in the form field. If provided, this enables first-frame control mode.
- "Last Frame Image" → last_frame_image parameter. Must be used WITH First Frame Image. When both are provided, this enables first+last frame control mode (video starts at first frame and ends at last frame). If only Last Frame Image is provided without First Frame Image, IGNORE it.
- "Aspect Ratio" → ratio parameter (9:16, 16:9, 4:3, 3:4, 1:1, or 21:9). Note: when images are provided, ratio is determined by the image and this parameter is ignored.
- "Duration" → duration parameter (extract the number: 4, 5, 6, 8, 10, 12, or 15)
- "Audio" → generate_audio parameter (true if "Yes", false if "No")

Image handling for form submissions:
- If the user uploaded photos from their library, they are sent as base64 data URIs in the images array. The FIRST image corresponds to "First Frame Image" and the SECOND image (if present) corresponds to "Last Frame Image".
- If the user pasted URLs in the text fields, use those URLs directly.
- If both a photo upload and URL exist for the same field, prefer the photo upload.

Call the sd_generate_video tool with these parameters. Video generation takes 1-3 minutes — this is normal.

After generation completes, the video file will be saved to the Desktop automatically. Confirm the result to the user with the file path and size.

IMPORTANT:
- Keep the prompt concise and visual — describe what the camera sees, not a script.
- If the prompt is too long, summarize it to under 500 characters before passing to the tool.
- Do NOT retry if the first attempt fails with a policy error — inform the user to modify their prompt.
- Do NOT call the tool more than once per request.
- The three image modes are MUTUALLY EXCLUSIVE: first_frame only, first+last frame, or reference_images. Never mix reference_images with first/last frame.
